overview.title: MYP Language & Literature Overview
overview.description: Select a phase from the diagram to explore, or click the centre circle to return to this overview.

sections.title: Programme Model
sections.quote: The MYP is flexible enough to accommodate the demands of most national or local curriculums.
sections.text: The programme is structured around concepts, global contexts and approaches to learning (ATL). Learning is inquiry-based and connected to real-world contexts.

sections.title: Nature of Language & Literature
sections.quote: Language is what makes us human. It is a recourse against the meaningless noise and silence of nature and history. — Octavio Paz
sections.text: Language is fundamental to learning, thinking and communicating; therefore it permeates the whole curriculum. All teachers are language teachers, continually expanding the boundaries of what students are thinking about. Students need to develop an appreciation of the nature of language and literature, of the many influences on language and literature, and of its power and beauty.

sections.title: Language, Literature and Inquiry
sections.quote: Literature is the art of discovering something extraordinary about ordinary people, and saying with ordinary words something extraordinary. — Boris Pasternak
sections.text: Language and literature incorporates creative processes and encourages imagination and creativity through self-expression. The six skill areas — listening, speaking, reading, writing, viewing and presenting — develop as both independent and interdependent skills within an inquiry-based learning environment. Students' interaction with texts generates insight into moral, social, economic, political, cultural and environmental factors, contributing to opinion-forming, decision-making and ethical-reasoning skills.

rows: Focus on identifying and responding to key aspects of texts.
Focus on explaining and interpreting texts and relationships between them.
Focus on analysing and evaluating texts with depth and sophistication.
Students engage with familiar texts and contexts.
Students engage with a broader range of texts across genres and contexts.
Students engage with complex texts across cultures, contexts and time periods.
Communication focuses on basic expression of ideas for familiar audiences.
Communication develops clarity and coherence for a wider range of audiences and purposes.
Communication is refined and effective across a wide range of contexts, audiences and purposes.
Creative and analytical responses are emerging and guided.
Creative and analytical responses show increasing independence and control.
Creative and analytical responses demonstrate insight, reflection and critical thinking.
Interpretation shows growing independence and justification.
Interpretation is critical, well-supported and evaluative.
Learning is guided through structured engagement with texts.
Learning increasingly involves independent interpretation and communication.
Learning is increasingly self-directed and critically reflective.

sections.title: Key and Related Concepts
sections.text: The key concepts contributed by the study of language and literature are communication, connections, creativity and perspective

key_definitions.name: Communication
key_definitions.definition: The exchange or transfer of signals, facts, ideas and symbols between a sender and receiver. In language and literature, communication involves expressing, analysing and transforming meaning through texts across contexts.

key_definitions.name: Connections
key_definitions.definition: Links and relationships among people, texts, ideas and cultures. Language and literature explores how texts connect across time, genres and contexts, shaping meaning and interpretation.

key_definitions.name: Creativity
key_definitions.definition: The process of generating new ideas and reinterpreting existing ideas from different perspectives. In language and literature, creativity emerges through the use of language to express, reflect and engage with ideas and audiences.

key_definitions.name: Perspective
key_definitions.definition: The position from which ideas, texts and meanings are viewed. Language and literature explores how perspectives influence interpretation, and how texts shape and challenge viewpoints.

items.name: Audience
items.definition: Who a text is created for, and how it is intended to affect or influence them.

items.name: Character
items.definition: How individuals are represented in texts through description, action and dialogue.

items.name: Context
items.definition: The social, cultural and historical conditions in which a text is created and interpreted.

items.name: Genre
items.definition: A category of texts with shared features, conventions and expectations.

items.name: Intertextuality
items.definition: The connections between texts and how meaning is shaped through these relationships.

items.name: Point of view
items.definition: The perspective from which a text is presented and interpreted.

items.name: Purpose
items.definition: The creator’s intention in producing a text and the message they aim to convey.

items.name: Self-expression
items.definition: The communication of personal ideas, feelings and perspectives through creative work.

items.name: Setting
items.definition: The time and place in which a text occurs, including its atmosphere.

items.name: Structure
items.definition: How a text is organised and how its parts relate to create meaning.

items.name: Style
items.definition: The distinctive use of language and techniques to achieve particular effects.

items.name: Theme
items.definition: The central idea or ideas explored within a text.

assessment_overview.intro: Each criterion has eight possible achievement levels (1–8), divided into four bands representing performance that is: limited (1–2), adequate (3–4), substantial (5–6), and excellent (7–8). Each band has its own unique descriptor used to make best-fit judgments.
assessment_overview.requirement: Subject groups must assess all strands of all four assessment criteria at least twice in each year of the MYP.

phases.label: Analysing
phases.description: Students are enabled to deconstruct texts in order to identify their essential elements and their meaning. Analysing involves demonstrating understanding of the creator's choices, the relationship between components of a text and between texts, and making inferences about how an audience responds to a text and the creator's purpose.

nodes.label: Analyse
nodes.detail: content, context, language, structure, technique and style
nodes.strand_full: Analyse the content, context, language, structure, technique and style of text(s) and the relationship among texts.
nodes.description: Students examine the full range of textual elements — what a text says, how it is constructed, the language and techniques used, and how it connects to other texts. Analysis requires more than identification; students must show how these elements work together to create meaning.

progression.y1: Identify and comment upon significant aspects of texts.
progression.y3: Identify and explain the content, context, language, structure, technique and style of text(s) and the relationship among texts.
progression.y5: Analyse the content, context, language, structure, technique and style of text(s) and the relationship among texts.

nodes.label: Analyse
nodes.detail: effects of the creator's choices on an audience
nodes.strand_full: Analyse the effects of the creator's choices on an audience.
nodes.description: Students examine why creators make specific choices — of form, language, structure, perspective — and how those choices position, affect or influence an intended audience. This moves analysis beyond describing what is present to explaining why it matters.

progression.y1: Identify and comment upon the creator’s choices.
progression.y3: Identify and explain the effects of the creator’s choices on an audience.
progression.y5: Analyse the effects of the creator’s choices on an audience.

nodes.label: Justify
nodes.strand_full: Justify opinions and ideas, using examples, explanations and terminology.
nodes.description: Students support their personal responses and interpretations with specific evidence from the text, clear explanations of how that evidence supports their view, and appropriate literary or linguistic terminology. Unsupported assertion does not constitute analysis.

progression.y1: Justify opinions and ideas using examples, explanations and terminology.
progression.y3: Justify opinions and ideas using examples, explanations and terminology.
progression.y5: Justify opinions and ideas using examples, explanations and terminology.

nodes.label: Evaluate
nodes.strand_full: Evaluate similarities and differences by connecting features across and within genres and texts.
nodes.description: Students engage in comparative analysis, identifying and evaluating how features — structural, thematic, stylistic — recur, differ or develop across and within genres and texts. Critical literacy requires awareness of different perspectives through interpretation.

progression.y1: Identify similarities and differences in features within and between texts.
progression.y3: Interpret similarities and differences in features within and between genres and texts.
progression.y5: Evaluate similarities and differences by connecting features across and within genres and texts.

y1.text: Provides minimal identification and comment upon significant aspects of texts; provides minimal identification and comment upon the creator's choices; rarely justifies opinions with examples or explanations and uses little or no terminology; identifies few similarities and differences in features within and between texts.

Provides adequate identification and comment upon significant aspects of texts; provides adequate identification and comment upon the creator's choices; justifies opinions with some examples and explanations though not consistently; uses some terminology; identifies some similarities and differences in features within and between texts.

Provides substantial identification and comment upon significant aspects of texts; provides substantial identification and comment upon the creator's choices; sufficiently justifies opinions with examples and explanations; uses accurate terminology; describes some similarities and differences in features within and between texts.

Provides perceptive identification and comment upon significant aspects of texts; provides perceptive identification and comment upon the creator's choices; gives detailed justification of opinions with a range of examples and thorough explanations; uses accurate terminology; compares and contrasts features within and between texts.

y3.text: Provides minimal identification or explanation of content, context, language, structure, technique and style, and does not explain the relationship among texts; provides minimal identification and explanation of the effects of the creator's choices; rarely justifies opinions and uses little or no terminology; interprets few similarities and differences in features within and between genres and texts.

Provides adequate identification and explanation of content, context, language, structure, technique and style, and some explanation of the relationship among texts; provides adequate identification and explanation of the effects of the creator's choices; justifies opinions with some examples and explanations though not consistently; uses some terminology; interprets some similarities and differences in features within and between genres and texts.

Provides substantial identification and explanation of content, context, language, structure, technique and style, and explains the relationship among texts; provides substantial identification and explanation of the effects of the creator's choices; sufficiently justifies opinions with examples and explanations; uses accurate terminology; competently interprets similarities and differences in features within and between genres and texts.

Provides perceptive identification and explanation of content, context, language, structure, technique and style, and explains the relationship among texts thoroughly; provides perceptive identification and explanation of the effects of the creator's choices; gives detailed justification with a range of examples and thorough explanations; uses accurate terminology; perceptively compares and contrasts features within and between genres and texts.

y5.text: Provides limited analysis of the content, context, language, structure, technique and style of text(s) and the relationship among texts; provides limited analysis of the effects of the creator's choices; rarely justifies opinions and uses little or no terminology; evaluates few similarities and differences with minimal connections across and within genres and texts.

Provides adequate analysis of content, context, language, structure, technique and style and the relationship among texts; provides adequate analysis of the effects of the creator's choices; justifies opinions with some examples and explanations though not consistently; uses some terminology; evaluates some similarities and differences with adequate connections across and within genres and texts.

Competently analyses content, context, language, structure, technique and style and the relationship among texts; competently analyses the effects of the creator's choices; sufficiently justifies opinions with examples and explanations; uses accurate terminology; evaluates similarities and differences with substantial connections across and within genres and texts.

Provides perceptive analysis of content, context, language, structure, technique and style and the relationship among texts; perceptively analyses the effects of the creator's choices; gives detailed justification with a range of examples and thorough explanations; uses accurate terminology; perceptively compares and contrasts with extensive connections across and within genres and texts.

phases.label: Organizing
phases.description: Students should understand and be able to organize their ideas and opinions using a range of appropriate conventions for different forms and purposes of communication. Students should also recognize the importance of maintaining academic honesty by respecting intellectual property rights and referencing all sources accurately.

nodes.label: Employ
nodes.strand_full: Employ organizational structures that serve the context and intention.
nodes.description: Students select and use structural approaches — paragraph organization, essay structure, narrative form, visual layout — that are appropriate to the text type, audience and purpose. Structure is a communicative choice, not a formula.

progression.y1: Employ organizational structures that serve the context and intention.
progression.y3: Employ organizational structures that serve the context and intention.
progression.y5: Employ organizational structures that serve the context and intention.

nodes.label: Organize
nodes.detail: ideas in a sustained, coherent and logical manner
nodes.strand_full: Organize opinions and ideas in a sustained, coherent and logical manner.
nodes.description: Students develop and sustain a line of argument or sequence of ideas that is internally consistent, logically sequenced and maintained throughout the text. Coherence requires each element to connect meaningfully to what precedes and follows it.

progression.y1: Organize opinions and ideas in a logical manner.
progression.y3: Organize opinions and ideas in a coherent and logical manner.
progression.y5: Organize opinions and ideas in a sustained, coherent and logical manner.

nodes.label: Use
nodes.strand_full: Use referencing and formatting tools to create a presentation style suitable to the context and intention.
nodes.description: Students apply appropriate referencing conventions and formatting choices to present their work in a way that serves the context and acknowledges the work of others. Academic honesty is embedded in this strand — all sources must be accurately attributed.

progression.y1: Use referencing and formatting tools to create a presentation style suitable to the context and intention.
progression.y3: Use referencing and formatting tools to create a presentation style suitable to the context and intention.
progression.y5: Use referencing and formatting tools to create a presentation style suitable to the context and intention.

y1.text: Makes minimal use of organizational structures that may not serve the context and intention; organizes opinions and ideas with minimal logic; makes minimal use of referencing and formatting tools and the presentation style may not be suitable.

Makes adequate use of organizational structures that serve the context and intention; organizes opinions and ideas with some logic; makes adequate use of referencing and formatting tools to create a suitable presentation style.

Makes competent use of organizational structures that serve the context and intention; organizes opinions and ideas in a logical manner with ideas building on each other; makes competent use of referencing and formatting tools to create a suitable presentation style.

Makes sophisticated use of organizational structures that serve the context and intention effectively; effectively organizes opinions and ideas in a logical manner with ideas building on each other in a sophisticated way; makes excellent use of referencing and formatting tools to create an effective presentation style.

y3.text: Makes minimal use of organizational structures that may not serve the context and intention; organizes opinions and ideas with minimal coherence and logic; makes minimal use of referencing and formatting tools and the presentation style may not be suitable.

Makes adequate use of organizational structures that serve the context and intention; organizes opinions and ideas with some coherence and logic; makes adequate use of referencing and formatting tools to create a suitable presentation style.

Makes competent use of organizational structures that serve the context and intention; organizes opinions and ideas in a coherent and logical manner with ideas building on each other; makes competent use of referencing and formatting tools to create a suitable presentation style.

Makes sophisticated use of organizational structures that serve the context and intention effectively; effectively organizes opinions and ideas in a coherent and logical manner with ideas building on each other in a sophisticated way; makes excellent use of referencing and formatting tools to create an effective presentation style.

y5.text: Makes minimal use of organizational structures that may not serve the context and intention; organizes opinions and ideas with minimal coherence and logic; makes minimal use of referencing and formatting tools and the presentation style may not be suitable.

Makes adequate use of organizational structures that serve the context and intention; organizes opinions and ideas with some coherence and logic; makes adequate use of referencing and formatting tools to create a suitable presentation style.

Makes competent use of organizational structures that serve the context and intention; organizes opinions and ideas in a coherent and logical manner with ideas building on each other; makes competent use of referencing and formatting tools to create a suitable presentation style.

Makes sophisticated use of organizational structures that serve the context and intention effectively; effectively organizes opinions and ideas in a sustained, coherent and logical manner with ideas building on each other in a sophisticated way; makes excellent use of referencing and formatting tools to create an effective presentation style.

phases.label:
Producing
Text
phases.description: Students produce written and spoken text, focusing on the creative process itself and on the understanding of the connection between the creator and the audience. Students develop the ability to make choices aimed at producing texts that affect both the creator and the audience.

nodes.label: Produce
nodes.detail: texts with insight, imagination and sensitivity
nodes.strand_full: Produce texts that demonstrate insight, imagination and sensitivity while exploring and reflecting critically on new perspectives and ideas arising from personal engagement with the creative process.
nodes.description: Students create original texts that show genuine personal engagement, imaginative thinking and sensitivity to ideas and perspectives. The creative process itself is valued — students reflect critically on how their choices emerge from and contribute to meaning.

progression.y1: Produce texts that demonstrate thought and imagination while exploring new perspectives and ideas arising from personal engagement with the creative process.
progression.y3: Produce texts that demonstrate thought, imagination and sensitivity while exploring and considering new perspectives and ideas arising from personal engagement with the creative process.
progression.y5: Produce texts that demonstrate insight, imagination and sensitivity while exploring and reflecting critically on new perspectives and ideas arising from personal engagement with the creative process.

nodes.label: Make
nodes.strand_full: Make stylistic choices in terms of linguistic, literary and visual devices, demonstrating awareness of impact on an audience.
nodes.description: Students deliberately select linguistic, literary and visual devices — figurative language, tone, imagery, layout, typography — with awareness of how those choices will affect the intended audience. Stylistic choices must be purposeful, not accidental.

progression.y1: Make stylistic choices in terms of linguistic, literary and visual devices, demonstrating awareness of impact on an audience.
progression.y3: Make stylistic choices in terms of linguistic, literary and visual devices, demonstrating awareness of impact on an audience.
progression.y5: Make stylistic choices in terms of linguistic, literary and visual devices, demonstrating awareness of impact on an audience.

nodes.label: Select
nodes.strand_full: Select relevant details and examples to develop ideas.
nodes.description: Students choose specific, relevant content — details, examples, evidence — that meaningfully develops their ideas rather than padding or restating. Selection is a craft skill that demonstrates both understanding and control of the text being produced.

progression.y1: Select relevant details and examples to support ideas.
progression.y3: Select relevant details and examples to develop ideas.
progression.y5: Select relevant details and examples to develop ideas.

y1.text: Produces texts with limited personal engagement, limited thought or imagination and minimal exploration of new perspectives; makes minimal stylistic choices demonstrating limited awareness of impact on an audience; selects few relevant details and examples to support ideas.

Produces texts with adequate personal engagement, some thought or imagination and some exploration of new perspectives; makes some stylistic choices demonstrating some awareness of impact on an audience; selects some relevant details and examples to support ideas.

Produces texts with considerable personal engagement, considerable thought or imagination and substantial exploration of new perspectives; makes thoughtful stylistic choices demonstrating good awareness of impact on an audience; selects sufficient relevant details and examples to support ideas.

Produces texts with a high degree of personal engagement, a high degree of thought or imagination and perceptive exploration of new perspectives; makes perceptive stylistic choices demonstrating clear awareness of impact on an audience; selects extensive relevant details and examples to support ideas.

y3.text: Produces texts with limited personal engagement, limited thought, imagination and sensitivity and minimal exploration and consideration of new perspectives; makes minimal stylistic choices demonstrating limited awareness of impact; selects few relevant details and examples to develop ideas.

Produces texts with adequate personal engagement, some thought, imagination and sensitivity and some exploration and consideration of new perspectives; makes some stylistic choices demonstrating adequate awareness of impact; selects some relevant details and examples to develop ideas.

Produces texts with considerable personal engagement, considerable thought, imagination and sensitivity and substantial exploration and consideration of new perspectives; makes thoughtful stylistic choices demonstrating good awareness of impact; selects sufficient relevant details and examples to develop ideas.

Produces texts with a high degree of personal engagement, a high degree of thought, imagination and sensitivity and perceptive exploration and consideration of new perspectives; makes perceptive stylistic choices demonstrating clear awareness of impact; selects extensive relevant details and examples to develop ideas with precision.

y5.text: Produces texts with limited personal engagement, limited insight, imagination and sensitivity and minimal exploration of, and critical reflection on, new perspectives; makes minimal stylistic choices demonstrating limited awareness of impact; selects few relevant details and examples to develop ideas.

Produces texts with adequate personal engagement, some insight, imagination and sensitivity and some exploration of, and critical reflection on, new perspectives; makes some stylistic choices demonstrating adequate awareness of impact; selects some relevant details and examples to develop ideas.

Produces texts with considerable personal engagement, considerable insight, imagination and sensitivity and substantial exploration of, and critical reflection on, new perspectives; makes thoughtful stylistic choices demonstrating good awareness of impact; selects sufficient relevant details and examples to develop ideas.

Produces texts with a high degree of personal engagement, a high degree of insight, imagination and sensitivity and perceptive exploration of, and critical reflection on, new perspectives; makes perceptive stylistic choices demonstrating good awareness of impact; selects extensive relevant details and examples to develop ideas with precision.

phases.label:
Using
Language
phases.description: Students have opportunities to develop, organize and express themselves and communicate thoughts, ideas and information. They are required to use accurate and varied language appropriate to the context and intention. This objective applies to written, oral and visual text, as appropriate.

nodes.label: Use
nodes.detail: appropriate and varied vocabulary and structures
nodes.strand_full: Use appropriate and varied vocabulary, sentence structures and forms of expression.
nodes.description: Students demonstrate command of a wide range of vocabulary and sentence structures suited to the context. Varied expression avoids repetition and shows linguistic flexibility — the ability to choose the right form for the right purpose.

progression.y1: Use appropriate and varied vocabulary, sentence structures and forms of expression.
progression.y3: Use appropriate and varied vocabulary, sentence structures and forms of expression.
progression.y5: Use appropriate and varied vocabulary, sentence structures and forms of expression.

nodes.label: Write and speak
nodes.strand_full: Write and speak in a register and style that serve the context and intention.
nodes.description: Students match their register — formal, informal, academic, creative — and stylistic approach to the demands of the communicative context and their intended purpose. Register awareness is fundamental to effective communication.

progression.y1: Write and speak in an appropriate register and style.
progression.y3: Write and speak in an appropriate register and style.
progression.y5: Write and speak in a register and style that serve the context and intention.

nodes.label: Use
nodes.strand_full: Use correct grammar, syntax and punctuation.
nodes.description: Students apply the grammatical conventions and syntactic structures of the language accurately. Correct grammar and punctuation are not merely mechanical — they shape meaning and affect how a text is received.

progression.y1: Use correct grammar, syntax and punctuation.
progression.y3: Use correct grammar, syntax and punctuation.
progression.y5: Use correct grammar, syntax and punctuation.

nodes.label: Spell and pronounce
nodes.strand_full: Spell (alphabetic languages), write (character languages) and pronounce with accuracy.
nodes.description: Students spell and pronounce words accurately in both written and oral contexts. Accuracy at word level is the foundation of clear communication and demonstrates command of the language system.

progression.y1: Spell, write and pronounce with accuracy.
progression.y3: Spell, write and pronounce with accuracy.
progression.y5: Spell, write and pronounce with accuracy.

nodes.label: Use
nodes.description: Students deploy non-verbal communication — gesture, eye contact, pacing, visual elements — appropriately in oral and visual texts. Non-verbal choices are part of the communicative act and contribute to audience impact.

y1.text: Uses a limited range of appropriate vocabulary and forms of expression; writes and speaks in an inappropriate register and style; uses grammar, syntax and punctuation with limited accuracy — errors often hinder communication; spells/writes and pronounces with limited accuracy — errors often hinder communication; makes limited and/or inappropriate use of non-verbal communication techniques.

Uses an adequate range of appropriate vocabulary, sentence structures and forms of expression; sometimes writes and speaks in a register and style that serve the context and intention; uses grammar, syntax and punctuation with some accuracy — errors sometimes hinder communication; spells/writes and pronounces with some accuracy — errors sometimes hinder communication; makes some use of appropriate non-verbal communication techniques.

Uses a varied range of appropriate vocabulary, sentence structures and forms of expression competently; writes and speaks competently in a register and style that serve the context and intention; uses grammar, syntax and punctuation with considerable accuracy — errors do not hinder effective communication; spells/writes and pronounces with considerable accuracy — errors do not hinder communication; makes sufficient use of appropriate non-verbal communication techniques.

Effectively uses a range of appropriate vocabulary, sentence structures and forms of expression; writes and speaks in a consistently appropriate register and style; uses grammar, syntax and punctuation with high accuracy — errors are minor and communication is effective; spells/writes and pronounces with high accuracy — errors are minor; makes effective use of appropriate non-verbal communication techniques.

y3.text: Uses a limited range of appropriate vocabulary and forms of expression; writes and speaks in an inappropriate register and style; uses grammar, syntax and punctuation with limited accuracy — errors often hinder communication; spells/writes and pronounces with limited accuracy — errors often hinder communication; makes limited and/or inappropriate use of non-verbal communication techniques.

Uses an adequate range of appropriate vocabulary, sentence structures and forms of expression; sometimes writes and speaks in a register and style that serve the context and intention; uses grammar, syntax and punctuation with some accuracy — errors sometimes hinder communication; spells/writes and pronounces with some accuracy — errors sometimes hinder communication; makes some use of appropriate non-verbal communication techniques.

Uses a varied range of appropriate vocabulary, sentence structures and forms of expression competently; writes and speaks competently in a register and style that serve the context and intention; uses grammar, syntax and punctuation with considerable accuracy — errors do not hinder effective communication; spells/writes and pronounces with considerable accuracy; makes sufficient use of appropriate non-verbal communication techniques.

Effectively uses a varied range of appropriate vocabulary, sentence structures and forms of expression; writes and speaks in a consistently appropriate register and style; uses grammar, syntax and punctuation with high accuracy — errors are minor; spells/writes and pronounces with high accuracy — errors are minor; makes effective use of appropriate non-verbal communication techniques.

y5.text: Uses a limited range of appropriate vocabulary and forms of expression; writes and speaks in an inappropriate register and style; uses grammar, syntax and punctuation with limited accuracy — errors often hinder communication; spells/writes and pronounces with limited accuracy — errors often hinder communication; makes limited and/or inappropriate use of non-verbal communication techniques.

Uses an adequate range of appropriate vocabulary, sentence structures and forms of expression; sometimes writes and speaks in a register and style that serve the context and intention; uses grammar, syntax and punctuation with some accuracy — errors sometimes hinder communication; spells/writes and pronounces with some accuracy — errors sometimes hinder communication; makes some use of appropriate non-verbal communication techniques.

Uses a varied range of appropriate vocabulary, sentence structures and forms of expression competently; writes and speaks competently in a register and style that serve the context and intention; uses grammar, syntax and punctuation with considerable accuracy — errors do not hinder effective communication; spells/writes and pronounces with considerable accuracy; makes sufficient use of appropriate non-verbal communication techniques.

Effectively uses a range of appropriate vocabulary, sentence structures and forms of expression; writes and speaks in a consistently appropriate register and style; uses grammar, syntax and punctuation with high accuracy — errors are minor; spells/writes and pronounces with high accuracy — errors are minor; makes effective use of appropriate non-verbal communication techniques.
